tt36352591.reason: A glossy rom-com with enough warmth to keep it in play.

la-belle-noiseuse.reason: Four-hour Rivette masterpiece about an artist painting his model - sounds boring, plays like a thriller.

utamaro-and-his-five-women.reason: Mizoguchi's gorgeous final film about the great woodblock artist and his complicated relationships with women.

hamlet.reason: Branagh's uncut four-hour version is the definitive Shakespeare adaptation - every subplot, every soliloquy, every moment.

louie-bluie.reason: Terry Zwigoff's documentary about eccentric blues musician Howard Armstrong is pure joy.

the-shop-around-the-corner.reason: Lubitsch's perfect romantic comedy that inspired You've Got Mail but is infinitely better.

the-third-man-1949.reason: Post-war Vienna, Orson Welles in the sewers, and that zither score - noir perfection.

kiss-me-deadly-1955.reason: Aldrich turns Mickey Spillane into atomic age paranoia with the most apocalyptic ending in noir.

sweet-smell-of-success-1957.reason: Burt Lancaster as a vicious columnist and Tony Curtis as his weasel publicist - New York cynicism at its sharpest.

no-other-choice.reason: 97% is stellar but I don't know this one - that score suggests it's worth the gamble.

three-colors-blue.reason: Kieslowski's meditation on grief through Binoche's devastating performance - the trilogy's emotional peak.

hokum-2026.reason: Adam Scott horror from the ODDITY director sounds promising but it's not out yet and could go either way.

tt0042593.reason: Bogart's most vulnerable performance as a volatile screenwriter suspected of murder - Nicholas Ray's masterpiece.

the-iron-giant.reason: Brad Bird's animated masterpiece about a boy and his robot is Spielberg-level heart with actual brains.

wall-e.reason: Pixar's environmental love story told mostly without dialogue is their most ambitious and successful film.

crumb.reason: Terry Zwigoff's portrait of underground cartoonist Robert Crumb and his deeply disturbed family is unforgettable.

the-breadwinner-2026-2026.reason: Nate Bargatze's standup is great but stay-at-home dad comedies are usually terrible - proceed with caution.

monty-python-and-the-holy-grail.reason: The coconut horses, the Black Knight, the killer rabbit - comedy doesn't get more quotable.

mile-end-kicks-2026.reason: Indie music love triangle with Barbie Ferreira sounds like it could be charming or insufferable.

steal-this-story-please-2026.reason: Amy Goodman documentary from solid filmmakers but journalism docs need more than good intentions.

fantastic-planet.reason: Surreal Czech animation about tiny humans and giant blue aliens - trippy sci-fi unlike anything else.

suzhou-river.reason: Jia Zhangke's dreamy Shanghai noir about obsession and identity is hypnotic and beautiful.

three-colors-white.reason: The trilogy's comedy entry about a Polish hairdresser's revenge is darker and funnier than expected.

rocco-and-his-brothers.reason: Visconti's epic about a family moving from Sicily to Milan is Italian neorealism at its most operatic.

camera-buff.reason: Kieslowski's early film about amateur filmmaker vs. authority is both personal and political.

tt0443706.reason: Fincher turns the unsolved case into a procedural masterpiece about obsession destroying lives.

barton-fink.reason: Coen Brothers' Hollywood nightmare with John Turturro as a playwright in writer's hell.

nostalghia.reason: Tarkovsky's meditation on exile and memory is gorgeous but glacially paced - know what you're signing up for.

let-the-sunshine-in.reason: Binoche navigates Parisian dating in Denis's relationship comedy - witty but slight.

john-wick.reason: Keanu avenges his puppy with ballet-like violence and world-building that spawned a franchise.

peppermint-candy.reason: Lee Chang-dong's backwards journey through one man's life in modern Korea is devastating.

the-rocky-horror-picture-show.reason: The midnight movie experience that invented audience participation. You go for the sing-alongs and fishnet stockings, not the plot.

inland-empire.reason: Three hours of Lynch's most inscrutable nightmare logic shot on digital video. Only if you're already deep in the David Lynch hole.

godzilla-vs-hedorah-1971.reason: The trippiest Godzilla movie ever made, complete with psychedelic musical numbers and an environmental message. Pure 70s kaiju weirdness.

over-your-dead-body-2026-2026.reason: Toxic couple tries to murder each other at a cabin retreat until worse people show up. Sounds like a solid B-movie if executed right.

kika.reason: Almodóvar's most divisive film is a candy-colored assault on good taste. You'll either love the chaos or walk out.

by-design-2026.reason: Woman becomes furniture to feel loved while Juliette Lewis narrates this body-swap fever dream. The premise is more interesting than the execution sounds.

hello-mary-lou-prom-night-ii-1987.reason: Death by computer and exploding Bibles make this the perfect 80s horror sequel. Michael Ironside seals the deal.

art-school-confidential.reason: Zwigoff's follow-up to Ghost World mistakes cynicism for insight. Even art school burnouts deserve better.

invention-for-destruction.reason: Zeman's steampunk masterpiece combines live action with incredible hand-drawn animation. Jules Verne brought to life like never before.

the-player.reason: Altman's razor-sharp takedown of Hollywood still cuts deep 30 years later. Tim Robbins has never been better.

chronicle-of-the-years-of-fire.reason: Lakhdar-Hamina's Palme d'Or winner about Algerian independence is an epic you've probably never heard of. Time to fix that.

goodbye-dragon-inn.reason: Tsai Ming-liang turns a closing movie theater into a meditation on loneliness and cinema itself. Glacially paced poetry.

tt0079944.reason: Tarkovsky's philosophical sci-fi about desire and faith in a mysterious Zone. Essential cinema that will haunt you for weeks.

the-headless-woman.reason: Martel's enigmatic thriller about guilt and class in Argentina unfolds like a fever dream. Every frame is perfectly composed.

vive-l-amour.reason: Tsai Ming-liang's lonely souls wander through modern Taipei in this quietly devastating portrait of urban alienation.

mad-bills-to-pay.reason: Don't know this one well enough to call it, but if it's screening at an art house it probably earned the spot.

no-picnic.reason: Another title I'm not familiar with. Check the venue and your tolerance for experimental work.

bridesmaids-15th-anniversary-2026.reason: Wiig and McCarthy's breakout comedy still holds up as the gold standard for R-rated female ensemble laughs.

donkey-days.reason: Not familiar with this one. Could be a hidden gem or festival filler.

variations-on-a-theme.reason: Title suggests experimental work but I can't make a solid call without more info.

two-mountains-weighing-down-my-chest.reason: The poetic title screams arthouse but I need more details to recommend a theater trip.

momma-s-man.reason: Jacobs' semi-autobiographical indie about a guy who can't leave his childhood home. Niche but potentially rewarding.

la-maison-des-bois.reason: French arthouse title I'm not familiar with. Check reviews if you're curious.

naked-childhood.reason: Pialat's brutal debut about a troubled kid in foster care launched the French New Wave's grittiest voice.

to-our-loves.reason: Pialat's raw family drama featuring his own daughter is uncomfortable and brilliant. Not easy viewing but unforgettable.

advance-screening-hokum-2026.reason: The Oddity director does witch horror with Adam Scott. Could be genuinely scary or just another cabin-in-the-woods retread.

michael-2026-2026.reason: Another sanitized music biopic that will probably skip the interesting parts of Jackson's story. Wait for streaming.

advance-screening-over-your-dead-body-20.reason: Same as the regular screening - toxic couple murder plot has potential if the execution delivers.

attack-on-titan-the-last-attack-subtitle.reason: The finale of anime's biggest series deserves the theater experience.

bridesmaids-15th-anniversary-movie-party.reason: Kristen Wiig's comedy holds up but anniversary screenings are for fans.

the-devil-wears-prada-2-costume-screenin.reason: Twenty years too late and probably soulless without the original's bite.

ferris-bueller-s-day-off-1986.reason: John Hughes perfection on the big screen never gets old.

fight-club-remaster-2026.reason: Fincher's masterpiece in 4K is exactly how toxic masculinity should be seen.

strange-journey-the-story-of-rocky-horro.reason: Doc about Rocky Horror could be great or just fan service.

mamma-mia-movie-party-2008.reason: Pierce Brosnan's singing is painful but the joy is infectious.

miss-congeniality-movie-party-2000.reason: Bullock's charm carries this formulaic but quotable crowd-pleaser.

shrek-25th-anniversary-movie-party-2026.reason: The animation revolution that killed Disney's stranglehold deserves respect.

the-big-lebowski-movie-party-1999.reason: The Coens' stoner noir is endlessly rewatchable cult perfection.

power-to-the-people-john-yoko-live-in-ny.reason: Lennon's only post-Beatles concerts, restored and legendary.

the-sheep-detectives-2026.reason: Hugh Jackman talking to detective sheep could be charming or insufferable.
